input.prompt: Return the full text content of this page, including the problem number, problem statement, any citations, references, variants, status information, and any other details. Reproduce everything as faithfully as possible.

content.content:
# Erdős Problem #93

**Status:** PROVED (LEAN) - Solved in the affirmative with proof verified in Lean.

**Problem Statement:**
If n distinct points in ℝ² form a convex polygon then they determine at least ⌊n/2⌋ distinct distances.

**Problem Number:** #93

**Original Sources:** [Er46b], [Er57], [Er61], [Er75f, p.100], [Er82e], [Er87b, p.175], [Er90], [Er92e], [Er95], [Er97e], [Er97f]

**Tags:** geometry, convex, distances

**History:**
The problem was "Solved by Altman [Al63]." However, a stronger variant remains open, asking whether some single point determines at least ⌊n/2⌋ distinct distances (see Problem 982).

**Related Conjectures:**
Fishburn conjectured that the sum of distinct distances from all points satisfies: "∑_{x∈A}R(x) ≥ (n choose 2)." Szemerédi proposed replacing convexity with the condition that no three points are collinear (Problem 1082).

**Related Problems:** Problem 660

**Additional Note:** A formalized statement does not currently exist on this platform.

content.text:
Here is the full text content from https://www.erdosproblems.com/93:

---

**Erdos Problem #93**

**Status:** PROVED (LEAN) -- Solved in the affirmative with proof verified in Lean.

**Problem Statement:** If n distinct points in R^2 form a convex polygon then they determine at least floor(n/2) distinct distances.

**Original Sources:** [Er46b], [Er57], [Er61], [Er75f, p.100], [Er82e], [Er87b, p.175], [Er90], [Er92e], [Er95], [Er97e], [Er97f]

**Tags:** geometry, convex, distances

**History:** The problem was solved by Altman [Al63]. However, a stronger variant remains open, asking whether some single point determines at least floor(n/2) distinct distances (see Problem 982).

**Related Conjectures:** Fishburn conjectured that the sum of distinct distances from all points satisfies a certain inequality. Szemerédi proposed replacing convexity with the condition that no three points are collinear (Problem 1082).

**Related Problems:** Problem 660

**Additional Note:** A formalized statement does not currently exist on this platform.
